literature: Collin-Dufresne Goldstein Martin 2001 (common factor in credit-spread changes)
credit-spread dispersion as a stress/credit-cycle gauge
sovereign-yield dispersion as a fragmentation gauge
meta.diversification_caveat: high PC1 share => segments move as one => cross-sectional diversification is illusory at the system level (self_marked_value U1-U4: 'gaps correlate under a common factor; no netting')

credit_oas.label: US corporate credit - option-adjusted spread (rating ladder + aggregates)
credit_oas.note: ICE BofA OAS by rating. Dispersion = the spread between quality buckets (credit discrimination).

sovereign_10y.label: Sovereign 10Y yield (global cross-section)
sovereign_10y.note: 23-country cross-section. Dispersion = sovereign fragmentation; PC1 = the global rates common factor.

muni_yield.label: Municipal bond distribution yield (per-state / quality ETFs)
muni_yield.note: Trailing-12mo distribution yield; dispersion across states & quality.

trace_breadth.label: Corporate breadth by quality tier (FINRA TRACE advance/decline ratio)
trace_breadth.note: Real TRACE tape: advancing/declining bonds by quality tier; PC1 = shared risk-on/off.

banks.label: Bank vulnerability cross-section (FDIC, per institution)
banks.note: Cross-sectional z per metric (higher=worse), composite = mean of the three; percentile vs peers.

dispersion_now.reading: how unequal the hidden HTM holes are across the system

graph_connectors.label: Funding-graph cross-layer connectors (bridging cross-section)
graph_connectors.note: bridge_score = z(degree) + z(distinct neighbor-sectors) + 0.5 if the node spans both layers.

unified.label: Unified cross-sectional snapshot (all segments, RV z-scored)
unified.note: Every segment's latest level z-scored vs its own history, so credit / sovereign / muni / breadth read side by side. Positive rv_z = wide/cheap vs own history (more stress priced).

rows.cross_section: Municipal bond distribution yield (per-state / quality ETFs)
